behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local key-derivation logic (normalization, ordering, hashing) within one function/object; no module handoff, real infrastructure, or model behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the key generator directly with parameter permutations (same semantic params in different orders, type-normalization cases, and adversarial distinct inputs) and assert deterministic equality/non-collision expectations.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "based on the data category's configured freshness rule". Slot B: handoff between config provider/category mapping and TTL computation. Slot C: lowering to Unit could miss a real wiring bug where category lookup returns the wrong rule or wrong time units after module integration. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess, real model output, or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test TTL service with real in-process config module + cache policy module: load category freshness config, request TTL for multiple categories, and assert resulting durations match configured minutes/hours exactly (including unit conversion boundaries).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires cross-module wiring (config -> TTL), which is the chosen failure boundary.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU". Slot B: handoff between memory-metrics input, threshold/config evaluation, and strategy selector. Slot C: lowering to Unit could miss miswiring where threshold events are produced but selector never flips strategy. Rejection gate: higher System level is rejected by behavior text because it does not require validating OS-level memory probes, only correct switch behavior from the provided usage signal.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test cache manager + memory-pressure module + config module in-process: drive usage below/above threshold and verify active strategy transitions LRU->LFU at the configured boundary and remains stable around hysteresis/edge values.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module state transition is central; pure unit tests of selector logic are insufficient.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries". Slot B: real-infra boundary (actual PostgreSQL write/event path to invalidation). Slot C: lowering to Integration with mocked DB/event stream could miss real failures in SQL trigger/notification payloads, transaction timing, or driver behavior that prevent invalidation.
behaviors.test_description: System-test with real PostgreSQL: seed rows and corresponding cache entries, perform actual table write/update/delete in Postgres, observe invalidation pipeline, and assert all keys matching affected rows are removed while unrelated keys remain.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior explicitly depends on real Postgres write effects, which requires System-level infrastructure.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "removes the correct entries ... under LRU ... under LFU ... when the cache is full". Slot B: handoff between access-tracking state (recency/frequency counters) and eviction executor. Slot C: lowering to Unit could miss real failures where counters are not updated by cache access path, so policy code is correct but wrong victim is evicted in the integrated cache.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test cache store + access tracker + eviction module: run controlled access sequences to create known recency/frequency ordering, fill cache to capacity under each mode, trigger eviction, and assert victim keys match expected LRU/LFU outcomes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module interaction (tracking + eviction), not just isolated policy function checks.
